text: Bible with you, Revelation chapter number four. We're looking at verses 10 and 11 this evening. And this is really five weeks or so that we've been in Revelation chapter number four. And I'm looking forward to, as brother Paul Mershan often says, not the undertaker, but the upper taker. And looking forward to hearing the trumpet of the Lord sound, amen. Listen for the trumpet. Jesus is coming, the songwriter said. And I believe that he could come in any moment. Looking forward to being able to see him face to face and spend eternity with him. We looked at the text of scripture beginning in verse number one of Revelation four. After this, after what? Well, that section of the book of Revelation part two, when he's writing about the churches, the seven churches in Asia and the things that are, and then the things that shall be hereafter. And we see after this, I looked and behold, a door was opened in heaven. And the first voice which I heard was as it were of a trumpet talking with me, which said, come up hither. And I will show thee things which must be hereafter. And immediately, John says, I was in the spirit and behold, a throne was set in heaven and one sat on the throne. And for the last several Sunday evenings, we talked about this glimpse that God gives us into the throne room of heaven. We dealt with the rapture, we dealt with the throne and him that sat upon it. What a blessing to understand that our God reigns, that he rules, that he is on his throne. We looked at the four and 20 elders. Last week, we looked at the four beast. And this evening, as we close out the chapter, I want you to notice in verse 10 and 11, the Bible says the four and 20 elders fall down before him. What causes them to fall down? Well, the beast, the four beast we mentioned last week in verse nine, and when those beasts give glory and honor and thanks to him that sat on the throne who liveth forever and ever, the four and 20 elders fall down before him that sat on the throne and worship him that liveth forever and ever and cast their crowns before the throne saying, notice what they say, thou art worthy, oh Lord, to receive glory and honor and power, for thou hast created all things and for thy pleasure they are and were created. I've titled the message this evening simply this, he is worthy to receive praise. As we look at this passage of scripture, we find this phrase in verse number 11, thou art worthy. We find in chapter number five, maybe on the same page for you, we see that phrase again in verse number nine of the fifth chapter, notice the Bible says they sung a new song saying, thou art worthy to take the book and to open the seals thereof for thou was slain and has redeemed us to God by thy blood out of every kindred and tongue and people and nation and has made us unto our God kings and priests and we shall reign on the earth. Isn't it wonderful what Jesus has done for us? And we find that as we look at this text of scripture to conclude the fourth chapter, thou art worthy, I wonder this evening, have you reached that conclusion in your life? Have you come to the place in your walk with God that you've acknowledged that he is worthy? You say he's worthy of what? Well, he's worthy of everything. He's worthy of my all and we find that as we come to the text of scripture and finish this scene in Revelation chapter number four, that the Bible says thou art worthy, O Lord, and then it says this, to receive, to receive. Now, we pause and consider the worthiness and the fact that we would use the phrase worship and in our English language, the word worship was originally used as worthship and somebody that was worthy and we worship our God, we find that in the throne room of heaven, there will be worship that will be taking place through the praise but also at the end of verse number 10 as they cast their crowns before the throne and we mentioned this a few weeks ago when we looked at the four and 20 elders and we exhausted a study on that but we find here this evening that we are entering into getting this glimpse after John is caught up, he hears that voice as a trumpet come up hither and I want you to remember, hold your place here but remember the truth of 2 Corinthians 5 and verse 15 as the Apostle Paul is writing to the believers in Corinth and I preached from this passage just a few months ago as we're going through the book of 2 Corinthians and the Bible tells us this in 2 Corinthians 5 and verse number 15 and that he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves but unto him which died for them and rose again and I just pause to think about that verse of scripture for a moment this evening and that he died for all. I'm thankful this evening that the Lord Jesus Christ died for all of humanity. I'm thankful that our God is a God so loved the world, God but the reality and I think we heard a piece of it this evening as the songwriter said, I am so glad that Jesus loves me and we can understand this evening that the truth of God loving the entire world and he's the propitiation for our sins but not for ours only but for the sins of the whole world and we understand this evening that he loves me as an individual, that he's concerned about me. Brother Mark this morning when he was in teaching in Sunday school, he started in John chapter number five and the Bible says that there's a great multitude of impotent folk that were lying there at Bethesda but you know what happens? The account in John chapter number five goes from a great multitude of impotent folk to a man that had an infirmity for 38 years and you go through the gospel records and you find repeatedly how that God deals with individuals this evening collectively, corporately, we ought to acknowledge that our God is worthy but really the question for us as individuals tonight is do we individually understand how worthy our God is? Do we understand how great he is? Do we understand that he's worthy of our praise? Listen, we should never be embarrassed to praise our God. We should never be embarrassed to speak about how wonderful and how gracious and how merciful and how great he is, why? Because we've tasted and we've seen that the Lord is good. We don't have to be ashamed, we don't have to be embarrassed. We can rejoice in knowing and declaring how great our God is, he is worthy and the Bible says this and I said that we'd get to it. Thou art worthy, O Lord. Notice what it says to receive. I titled the message he is worthy to receive praise. We see that the phraseology of verse number 11 to receive glory and honor and power but I want you to think about that word receive just for a moment. So often in the Christian life when we talk about receiving we talk about us being on the end of receiving, that we are the receiver. We often think about and I quote often from John chapter number one where the Bible says that he came to his own and his own received him not but as many as received him to them gave he power to become the sons of God even to them that believe on his name. We could ask the question this evening have you received the gift of eternal life and often when we talk about receiving we're on that receiving end and we understand that the gift of God is made available to us through his sacrificial substitutionary death on the cross of Calvary. I'm thankful that it's finished. He paid it all and he offers to us this free gift of eternal life. I've received it. I know by personal testimony that many of you have shared with me that you too have received the gift of eternal life and we're heaven bound because of it but I want you to notice in the text of scripture that it is not the saved individual, it's not the redeemed that are on the end of receiving. It is God who is receiving. The Bible says thou art worthy O Lord to receive. How does he receive it? Somebody's gotta give it, amen. Somebody's gotta give it. Now I know that the angels, we read about the four beasts, those angels we mentioned last week, seraphims and cherubims that we see as Ezekiel would give us that glimpse and Isaiah in Isaiah chapter number six, the seraphims and what are they doing? They're crying out holy, holy, holy. They're exalting and magnifying the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and we see that as we enter into Revelation four and five and what a scene it is but the reality this evening is that I wonder as I examine my life, as you examine your life, what's he receiving from me? What's he receiving from us? Because he's worthy to receive. But I. I just wonder tonight, as we look at this passage of scripture and we consider that our God is to receive praise, I wonder how often he hears praise from his people. They had experienced times with no water. And did not God provide for them? I mean, they saw the power of God displayed for them. They received God's provision. And they're murmuring.
